steps.title: Identify the control problem
steps.content: To measure the effect of holding office, researchers need to compare officeholders with non-officeholders who are otherwise similar. But researchers cannot control who wins elections, so they cannot easily create the ideal comparison group.

steps.title: Keep the conclusion narrow
steps.content: A shifts from finding comparable people to finding data. B invents a rule that only former officeholders can be studied. C directly contradicts the requirement that the control group be similar to officeholders. The sentence should conclude that an appropriate control group will be hard to identify. The answer is D.
